SEARCH
Package com.tngtech.archunit.base

Class DescribedPredicate<T>

java.lang.Object
com.tngtech.archunit.base.DescribedPredicate<T>
Type Parameters:
T - The type of objects the predicate applies to
All Implemented Interfaces:
Predicate<T>
Direct Known Subclasses:
PackageMatchers
@PublicAPI(usage=INHERITANCE) public abstract class DescribedPredicate<T> extends Object implements Predicate<T>
A predicate holding a description.

  • Constructor Details

    • DescribedPredicate

      public DescribedPredicate(String description, Object... params)

  • Method Details

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()

    • as

      public DescribedPredicate<T> as(String description, Object... params)
      Overwrites the description of this DescribedPredicate. E.g. 
      
 classes().that(predicate.as("some customized description with '%s'", "parameter")).should().bePublic()
      would then yield classes that some customized description with 'parameter' should be public.
      Parameters:
      description - The new description of this DescribedPredicate
      params - Optional arguments to fill into the description via String.format(String, Object...)
      Returns:
      An DescribedPredicate with adjusted description.

    • and

      public DescribedPredicate<T> and(DescribedPredicate<? super T> other)

    • or

      public DescribedPredicate<T> or(DescribedPredicate<? super T> other)

    • onResultOf

      public <F> DescribedPredicate<F> onResultOf(Function<? super F,? extends T> function)

    • forSubtype

      public final <U extends T> DescribedPredicate<U> forSubtype()
      Convenience method to downcast the predicate. DescribedPredicates are contravariant by nature, i.e. an DescribedPredicate<T> is an instance of DescribedPredicate<V>, if and only if V is an instance of T.
      Take for example Object > String. Obviously a DescribedPredicate<Object> is also a DescribedPredicate<String>.
      Unfortunately, the Java type system does not allow us to express this property of the type parameter of DescribedPredicate. So to avoid forcing users to cast everywhere it is possible to use this method which also documents the intention and reasoning.
      Type Parameters:
      U - A subtype of the DescribedPredicate's type parameter T
      Returns:
      A DescribedPredicate accepting a subtype of the predicate's actual type parameter T

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<T> doNot(DescribedPredicate<? super T> predicate)
      Same as not(DescribedPredicate) but with a different description

    • not

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<T> not(DescribedPredicate<? super T> predicate)
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of object the predicate applies to
      Parameters:
      predicate - Any DescribedPredicate
      Returns:
      A predicate that will return true whenever the original predicate would return false and vice versa.

    • and

      @SafeVarargs @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<T> and(DescribedPredicate<? super T>... predicates)
      See Also:
      • and(Iterable)

    • and

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<T> and(Iterable<? extends DescribedPredicate<? super T>> predicates)
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of object the predicate applies to
      Parameters:
      predicates - Any number of predicates to join together via *AND*
      Returns:
      A predicate that returns true, if all the supplied predicates return true. Otherwise, it returns false. If an empty Iterable is passed to this method the resulting predicate will always return false.

    • or

      @SafeVarargs @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<T> or(DescribedPredicate<? super T>... predicates)
      See Also:
      • or(Iterable)

    • or

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<T> or(Iterable<? extends DescribedPredicate<? super T>> predicates)
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of object the predicate applies to
      Parameters:
      predicates - Any number of predicates to join together via *OR*
      Returns:
      A predicate that returns true, if any of the supplied predicates returns true. Otherwise, it returns false. If an empty Iterable is passed to this method the resulting predicate will always return false.

    • empty

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static DescribedPredicate<Iterable<?>> empty()

    • optionalContains

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<Optional<T>> optionalContains(DescribedPredicate<? super T> predicate)

    • optionalEmpty

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<Optional<T>> optionalEmpty()

    • anyElementThat

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<Iterable<? extends T>> anyElementThat(DescribedPredicate<? super T> predicate)
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of object the predicate applies to
      Parameters:
      predicate - A DescribedPredicate for the elements of the Iterable
      Returns:
      A predicate for an Iterable that returns true if and only if at least one element of the Iterable matches the given element predicate.

    • allElements

      @PublicAPI(usage=ACCESS) public static <T> DescribedPredicate<Iterable<T>> allElements(DescribedPredicate<? super T> predicate)
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of object the predicate applies to
      Parameters:
      predicate - A DescribedPredicate for the elements of the Iterable
      Returns:
      A predicate for an Iterable that returns true if and only if all elements of the Iterable matche the given element predicate.
